$comment: Canonical legal-transition tables for the Warp Commerce Model state machines. This file is the SINGLE data source for transition validity in every language binding (TypeScript @warp-lang/commerce-types and Python warp-commerce-types both read these edges), so the bindings agree by construction. Every (from -> to) pair NOT listed here is rejected. Enforces Invariant 2 (State Monotonicity).

commitment.doc: The 26 valid commitment transitions. The Modified -> Accepted loop and Tendered -> Accepted (auction completion) are explicit. Cancelled and Refunded are terminal.

intent.doc: An Active intent may be abandoned, converted, or expire. All non-Active states are terminal.

fulfillment.doc: Fulfillment lifecycle. Failed is terminal in the plain table; Failed -> Planned is allowed ONLY when the failure was recoverable (see special_cases).

special_cases.doc: A Failed fulfillment may retry to Planned only if its `recoverable` flag is true. This is the only edge gated on a payload field rather than the state type alone.

history.doc: Every applied transition appends one entry to the object's history; history is never rewritten and entry timestamps never move backward (equal is allowed). Enforces Invariant 4 (Temporal Integrity).
